chunk_summary: Statutory energy and protein norms under the PM POSHAN scheme ensure school-age children receive adequate nutrition through mid-day meals, with specific caloric and protein standards for lower and upper primary classes.
learning_objectives: Understand statutory energy and protein norms for PM POSHAN school meals as per NFSA Schedule II.
Analyze the impact of missed meal-days and partial consumption on pediatric nutrition outcomes.
Identify key audit fields for school meal service and consumption in public health reviews.
key_facts: Lower primary students are entitled to 450 kcal and 12 g protein per school day.
Upper primary students are entitled to 700 kcal and 20 g protein per school day.
Entitlement includes one free meal per school day, excluding holidays.
Portion size errors and plate waste have a larger impact on upper primary students due to higher nutrient density requirements.
Proximal factors like handwashing and safe drinking water influence meal consumption behavior.
reference_quote: Schedule II standards specify that lower primary classes should receive a hot cooked meal providing about 450 kcal and 12 g protein per child per school day, while upper primary classes should receive about 700 kcal and 20 g protein per child per school day.
document_key_topics: Statutory energy and protein norms for school meals
Impact of meal service interruptions on nutrition
Audit fields for school meal implementation
